title: run (deprecated)
text: run (deprecated) Starts the specified Form. Important: The run command is deprecated. It has been superseded by activate , which provides more functionality and flexibility. For more information, see activate . run { /display | /query } FormName { , VerticalPosition , HorizontalPosition { , VerticalSize , HorizontalSize }} Qualifiers Table 1. Qualifiers Qualifier Description /display Displays the Form as read-only. /query Displays the Form as retrieve-only; that is, data can be entered into the fields, but only for a retrieve profile. Using the /query switch on the run statement is equivalent to setting the property QUERY=TRUE component instance property. Parameters Table 2. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description FormDefinition String Name of Form file specification (maximim of 32 characters). If FormDefinition does not include the complete file specification and the Form is not located using an assignment, Uniface looks for the requested Form in the current directory. VerticalPosition , HorizontalPosition , VerticalSize and HorizontalSize String Position and size of the Form, expressed as integers representing character cells from the upper left corner of the application window. Return Values Sets $status to the value returned by the exec operation of the Form that was started, if that trigger contains a return or exit statement. In this case, $procerror is 0. The default values returned by run (that is, if no return or exit statement is present) are shown in this table. Table 3. Values returned in $status Value Meaning 10 The user used ^QUIT to leave the Form that was started with run . 9 The user used ^ACCEPT to leave the Form that was started with run . 0 The Form did not contain an edit or display statement in its exec operation. <0 An error occurred. $procerror contains the exact error. -1 FormSpec could not be found, or there is already a Form with this name in the Form stack. -3 The Form is a non-modal Form and cannot be started with run . Use newinstance (or activate ) instead. Table 4. Values commonly returned by $procerror following run Value Error constant Meaning -58 <UACTERR_NO_COMPONENT> The named component cannot be found. This error is also returned in case a modal Form is started using run , and the Form is already running. -154 <UACTERR_INSTANCE_NAME_EXISTS> An instance with this name already exists. This error code is returned, for example, when: A modal Form which is already active is activated again An attempt is made to activate a modal Form from a non-modal Form -1106 <UPROCERR_COMPONENT> The component name provided is not valid; for example, the argument contains an empty string (""). Use Allowed in Form, Report, and Service components, but not in self-contained components. Also allowed in the apStart trigger of Application Shells. Description The run statement loads the Form named by FormDefinition and executes the statements in the exec operation. After the Form has been loaded, the Form is known by its name in the Repository, excluding any file specification provided in FormDefinition . For example, if the Form is started by the following statement, the function $formname returns the name of the Form, QRY1 : run "c:/t_forms/qry1.frm" Copy Restrictions Using run The activate statement provides the same functionality as run , but provides more possibilities for the component being started. Forms started with run have the following restrictions: They are always modal and, thus, attached. They do not offer a callable interface that allows operations to be activated and parameters to be passed. They cannot receive messages sent with postmessage . They are not considered component instances, so functions such as $componentname and $instancename return the Form name, and $instanceparent returns an empty string. Labels with $text When there are labels on the Form that are filled with $text , the values of $language and $variation when the run statement is encountered determine the language and library that are used for these labels. If ProcScript in the exec operation of the Form changes the values of these functions, the changes are not reflected in the labels, since the labels are loaded before the exec operation is activated. You can override this behavior to some extent by setting the Form's Component Behavior property to Drop component definition from memory . In this case, the Form is not maintained in the form stack; the language and library are re-established each time the Form is loaded. Use of Functions When referring to a Form created with run : $formname returns the name of the Form. $formdb , $formdbmod , and $formmod return various modification statuses associated with the Form. $componentname , $componenttype , $instancechildren , $instancename , and $instanceparent return an empty string (""). $instancedb , $instancedbmod , and $instancemod return -1. These functions are meaningful only in component instances started (explicitly or implicitly) with the newinstance statement. Related Links activate Calls an operation on a component instance.
